title: foundation — domain events
description: Five framework-level events. All fire synchronously (NOT ShouldDispatchAfterCommit) because they carry the boot + module + config + health signals that downstream listeners MUST observe in order. Listeners subscribe via each module's own listeners.json.

events.name: ApplicationBooted
events.when: Fires after Laravel's service-provider chain completes + every ModuleServiceProvider has booted + the ModuleRegistry is fully resolved. Signal to downstream listeners that the platform is ready to serve traffic.

payload.applicationId: string|null (resolved from host, null for CLI + queue workers)

events.name: ModuleDiscovered
events.when: Fires per-module during ModuleRegistry's discovery pass, BEFORE the module's service provider boots. Used by observability + audit modules to record which modules loaded in which order.

events.name: ModuleBooted
events.when: Fires per-module AFTER the module's service provider register + boot phases complete + all contributions (traits, middleware, events, hooks, ...) are registered. Downstream modules can subscribe to know when a dependency finished booting (rare — usually the DI container handles this).

payload.contributions: array (traits: n, middleware: n, events: n, hooks: n, jobs: n, ...)

events.name: HealthProbeFailed
events.when: Fires when a health probe returns a failure status. Downstream observability + alerting modules subscribe to route these to the correct channels (PagerDuty for critical failures, Slack for non-critical).

events.name: ConfigReloaded
events.when: Fires when config is hot-reloaded via `POST /api/v1/platform/config/reload` OR when the config cache is regenerated on deploy. Modules with cached config drop their caches on receipt.

payload.keys: array<string> — which config keys changed (empty = full reload)
